skill.name: claudeemailskill
skill.description: Guides AI agents through Claude-oriented email prompts, campaign drafts, critique rubrics, rewrite loops, and approval-ready email handoffs.
skill.summary: Plan, draft, review, and QA Claude-assisted email work with clear human approval gates.
skill.author: Sequenzy

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged five weak cryptography patterns, but the flagged lines contain ordinary prose such as descriptions, designed workflows, and destructive cleanup warnings. No cryptographic code, scripts, network access, filesystem access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior was found in the reviewed files.

content.value_statement: Email teams need clear prompts, review standards, and approval gates before AI-assisted copy reaches production. This skill structures Claude email work into briefs, drafts, critique rubrics, rewrites, and QA handoffs.
actual_capabilities: Turns email requests into briefs with audience, CTA, tone, constraints, and forbidden claims.
Chooses Claude task shapes for ideation, drafting, critique, rewriting, variants, or final QA.
Builds critique rubrics based on relevance, specificity, risk, readability, and production readiness.
Guides rewrite loops while preserving approved facts and changing only requested dimensions.
Marks items that need human approval, legal review, data validation, or ESP testing.
limitations: Does not send emails or make live production changes.
Does not verify claims beyond the source material provided by the user.
Does not replace legal, compliance, deliverability, or brand approval.
Does not manage contacts, suppression lists, DNS settings, or ESP migrations.

use_cases.title: Campaign Briefing
use_cases.description: Create a structured brief before drafting a launch, nurture, or lifecycle email.

use_cases.title: Copy Review
use_cases.description: Evaluate a draft against source claims, audience fit, CTA clarity, and approval needs.

use_cases.title: Agent Handoff
use_cases.description: Prepare Claude Code or Codex handoffs with clear QA notes and production boundaries.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Email Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for planning a single email.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to turn my email request into a brief. Include audience, promise, proof, CTA, tone, constraints, and approval needs.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Campaign Email
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for producing reviewable copy.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to draft a campaign email from my source notes. Include subject, preview, body, CTA, and QA notes.

prompt_templates.title: Review and Rewrite
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for critique and controlled revision.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to critique this email against the brief, then rewrite it while preserving approved facts and changing only tone and length.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Variant Packet
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for testing and approval handoff.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create three meaningful variants, explain each test hypothesis, flag risk, and prepare an approval-ready handoff.

output_examples.input: Plan a product update email for current customers.
output_examples.output: A concise brief with audience, message promise, proof points, CTA, tone limits, forbidden claims, and required reviewers.

output_examples.input: Review this draft before it goes to legal.
output: A rubric-based critique tied to the brief.
A list of unsupported claims and risky wording.
A revised draft with approval notes.

output_examples.input: Create variants for a reactivation campaign.
output_examples.output: A variant set with subject lines, preview text, body copy, CTA emphasis, test rationale, and QA warnings.

best_practices: Provide source claims, audience details, tone examples, and forbidden language before drafting.
Ask for critique against a rubric before requesting final copy.
Require explicit human approval before live sends or production email changes.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the agent to invent proof, pricing, guarantees, or compliance claims.
Do not treat draft copy as approved production content.
Do not combine copywriting with live ESP actions in one unsupervised request.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps agents plan, draft, review, rewrite, and QA Claude-assisted email work.

faq.question: Can it send emails?
faq.answer: No. The skill separates recommendations from live actions and requires explicit approval for high-risk changes.

faq.question: Does it work with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports Codex, Claude, and Claude Code handoffs for structured email workflows.

faq.question: What inputs should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide audience, offer, source claims, CTA, tone examples, constraints, and required reviewers.

faq.question: Can it review existing copy?
faq.answer: Yes. It can critique drafts against a brief and suggest controlled rewrites.

faq.question: Does it replace legal review?
faq.answer: No. It flags approval needs but does not replace legal, compliance, or brand review.
